chosen_action.title: Repair broken first-run docs path and restore the Codeberg-first conversion route

why_this_action.summary: Adoption is flat, external outreach lanes are already saturated or inside live measurement windows, and the current first-run conversion path was visibly broken: README linked to a missing docs map, START_HERE linked to a missing first-task guide, and the workspace license/docs route was incomplete. Repairing the broken path is a higher-leverage move than adding another overlapping outreach touch.

verification: Created docs/README.md to restore the docs switchboard target
Created docs/first-task-guide.md to restore the task-selection target
Patched README.md and START_HERE.md so their local routes resolve cleanly
Copied LICENSE into the workspace root so the README license link resolves
Local markdown link check passed for README.md, START_HERE.md, docs/README.md, and docs/first-task-guide.md

expected_outcome: A cleaner Codeberg-first first-run path that reduces evaluator drop-off and gives current/future repo visitors a working route from interest to first task selection.

measurement_window.success_metric: Any Codeberg adoption delta, improved first-run feedback quality, or reduced broken-path friction in the next review window.
measurement_window.kill_condition: If Codeberg is still flat by May 31, 2026, treat this repair as necessary but insufficient and pair the next run with a new high-intent demand-capture lane instead of another docs-only pass.

docs_review_note.what_changed: Added the missing docs switchboard and first-task guide, then pruned broken workspace-only operator-manual links from top-level entry pages.
docs_review_note.why_here: These belong on top-level conversion surfaces because README and START_HERE were already sending evaluators there; leaving those links broken directly hurts first-run conversion.
docs_review_note.pruned_or_shortened: Removed the dead operator-manual pointer from the top-level workspace path and replaced it with a working first-task path.
docs_review_note.why_top_level_is_better_now: The first screen now leads to working pages that answer what to try first and how to judge it, instead of dropping evaluators onto missing files.
